about.title: About eatcreami
about.intro: eatcreami is a platform dedicated to helping Ninja Creami owners make the most of their machines. Whether you're a beginner making your first pint or an experienced creator experimenting with new flavors, you'll find tested recipes with step-by-step guidance.
about.stepByStepDesc: Every recipe walks you through the process with clear instructions and pro tips from experienced Creami users.
about.cookModeDesc: A focused, one-step-at-a-time view perfect for following along in the kitchen.
about.ratingsDesc: Real reviews from real Creami owners help you find the best recipes.
about.modelCompatDesc: Know which Creami model each recipe is designed for — Original, Deluxe, or Breeze.
about.joinDesc: Explore our curated collection of tested recipes with detailed instructions, pro tips, and model compatibility for every Ninja Creami owner.
about.faq1a: The Ninja Creami is a countertop appliance that transforms frozen solid bases into ice cream, sorbet, gelato, frozen yogurt, and more using a powerful blade that shaves and churns the frozen mixture into a creamy texture.
about.faq2q: Which Ninja Creami model should I buy?
about.faq2a: The Original (16oz pints) is great for beginners. The Deluxe (24oz pints) offers larger servings and extra programs like Gelato and Italian Ice. The Breeze is the budget-friendly option with core features.
about.faq3q: How long do I need to freeze the base?
about.faq3a: Most recipes require 24 hours of freezing. Some cream cheese-based recipes can work in as little as 3 hours. Always freeze on a flat, level surface for even results.
about.faq4q: Why is my Creami ice cream crumbly?
about.faq4a: This is normal after the first spin. Simply select Re-Spin for a smoother texture. Protein-based recipes often need 2-3 re-spins. If still crumbly, let the pint sit at room temperature for 3-5 minutes and try again.
about.faq5q: Can I make vegan ice cream in a Ninja Creami?
about.faq5a: Yes! Full-fat coconut milk, oat milk, and almond milk all make excellent dairy-free bases. Check our Vegan and Dairy-Free recipe categories for tested options.
about.faq6q: What's the best base for beginners?
about.faq6a: A simple vanilla ice cream with heavy cream, milk, sugar, and vanilla is the perfect first recipe. It teaches you the basics and always turns out great.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: eatcreami is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains how we handle your information when you visit our website.
privacy.collectDesc: We collect minimal information to provide and improve our service:
privacy.collectCookies: Cookies — We use a locale preference cookie to remember your language choice and a measurement system cookie for ingredient display. These are functional cookies only.
privacy.collectAnalytics: Analytics — We may use privacy-respecting analytics to understand how visitors use the site. No personal data is sold or shared with third parties.
privacy.collectAds: Advertising — When ads are displayed, our ad partners may use cookies to serve relevant advertisements. You can opt out through your browser settings.
privacy.noAccountDesc: We do not require user accounts or collect personal information such as email addresses, names, or passwords. There is no login system on this site.
privacy.thirdPartyDesc: Our site may include links to external websites (such as Amazon for product recommendations). These sites have their own privacy policies, and we are not responsible for their practices.
privacy.changesDesc: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated date.
privacy.contactDesc: If you have questions about this privacy policy, please reach out through our About page.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: By using eatcreami, you agree to these terms. Please read them carefully.
terms.useDesc: eatcreami provides recipe content for informational purposes. Recipes are for personal, non-commercial use. You may share links to recipes but may not republish recipe content in bulk without permission.
terms.disclaimerDesc: All recipes are provided as-is. Results may vary depending on your specific Ninja Creami model, ingredients, and technique. Always check ingredient labels for allergens. We are not responsible for adverse reactions or unsatisfactory results.
terms.ipDesc: Recipe instructions, descriptions, photography, and site design are the property of eatcreami. Ingredient lists and basic cooking facts are not copyrightable and may be freely referenced.
terms.affiliateDesc: Some links on this site may be affiliate links. This means we may earn a small commission if you make a purchase through these links, at no additional cost to you. This helps support the site.
terms.liabilityDesc: eatcreami is not liable for any damages arising from the use of recipes or information on this site. Use the site at your own risk.
terms.changesDesc: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
